# UX Data Performance API Security Checklist: Drill Alert Details and Context

**Purpose**: Validate the quality of requirements across UX, data, performance, API, and security concerns for UC-12 before implementation work begins.
**Created**: 2026-03-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](/Users/sahmed/Documents/311-forecast-system/specs/012-uc-12-drill-alert-details/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the operator-facing review needs defined beyond simply opening an alert, including what information must remain visible while loading and what context must appear for rendered, partial, and error outcomes? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK002 Are the required alert-detail components fully defined, including forecast distribution, top 5 ranked drivers, anomaly context from the previous 7 days, and the selected alert metadata that ties them together? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002 through §FR-008, Spec §Key Entities, Data Model §AlertDetailView]
- [x] CHK003 Are the allowed supporting-component outcomes fully specified, including `available`, `unavailable`, and `failed`, rather than leaving partial-view behavior to implementation inference? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010 through §FR-015, Plan §API Contract Direction, Data Model §Component Status]
- [x] CHK004 Are all API consumers and interactions defined, including authenticated detail retrieval and authenticated reporting of final render outcomes, rather than only the end-user drill-down action? [Completeness, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §paths]
- [x] CHK005 Does the specification define what performance expectations apply to successful, partial, and error detail loads, or is “quickly enough for interactive investigation” still left qualitative? [Gap, Spec §Success Criteria, Plan §Performance Goals]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is “open an alert-detail context that identifies the selected alert” precise about which alert metadata must stay visible before supporting components finish loading? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK007 Is “related to the selected alert” unambiguous for distribution, driver, and anomaly retrieval, including how alert scope, time context, and source lineage are resolved? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003 through §FR-005, Plan §Service Direction, Data Model §Overview]
- [x] CHK008 Is “combine the data into a visualization-ready form” specific enough to distinguish backend normalization rules from frontend rendering responsibilities? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, Plan §Summary, Plan §Service Direction, Data Model §AlertDetailView]
- [x] CHK009 Is the distinction between `unavailable` and `failed` clear enough that teams will implement the same UX, data, and API behavior for each outcome? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010 through §FR-015, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §Derived Invariants]
- [x] CHK010 Is “clear error state” explicit enough to prevent conflicting interpretations about whether any previously retrieved components may still be shown after a retrieval or render failure? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-015, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §Implementation Notes]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Are the requirements for partial-context display consistent between user stories, edge cases, functional requirements, the plan, and the data model’s `partial` view semantics? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-013a, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §AlertDetailView]
- [x] CHK012 Are failure-handling rules consistent between the spec’s error-state wording and the plan or data-model rule that any component `failed`, preparation failure, or render failure forces the overall view to `error`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-015, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §AlertDetailLoadRecord, Data Model §Derived Invariants]
- [x] CHK013 Are the accepted clarifications for top 5 drivers and previous 7-day anomalies used consistently across the spec, plan, data model, and API contract? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005, Data Model §DriverAttributionContext, Data Model §AnomalyContextWindow, Contract §components.schemas]
- [x] CHK014 Are assumptions about supported alert sources consistent between the spec’s generic “alert” language and the plan or contract vocabulary limited to `threshold_alert` and `surge_alert`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001, Plan §API Contract Direction, Data Model §Alert Source, Contract §AlertSource]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can the success criteria objectively verify UX value for operational managers, or do they only confirm that components and logs exist after a drill-down attempt? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [x] CHK016 Are performance targets measurable for the critical interactive path, including backend detail retrieval and final client render reporting, rather than only stakeholder-observed correctness? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001 through §SC-004, Plan §Performance Goals, Contract §POST /api/v1/alert-details/{alertDetailLoadId}/render-events]
- [x] CHK017 Can the requirement that successful and failed drill-downs be traceable be objectively verified without a more explicit definition of required identifiers, timestamps, and retained failure details? [Measurability, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-016, Spec §SC-003, Spec §SC-004, Data Model §AlertDetailLoadRecord]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are primary UX scenarios defined for fully successful drill-down, partial-context drill-down, and total failure states from selection through visible outcome? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories, Plan §Summary, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK019 Are alternate data scenarios specified for cases where upstream alert lineage resolves correctly but one supporting component has no reliable data to return? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §FR-010 through §FR-013a, Data Model §ForecastDistributionContext, Data Model §DriverAttributionContext, Data Model §AnomalyContextWindow]
- [x] CHK020 Are recovery requirements defined for what happens after a render failure is reported, such as whether the same load record can be retried or whether a new drill-down attempt must be created? [Coverage, Recovery Flow, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Data Model §AlertDetailLoadRecord, Contract §POST /api/v1/alert-details/{alertDetailLoadId}/render-events]
- [x] CHK021 Are API error and authorization scenarios covered by written requirements for unauthenticated access, unauthorized roles, missing alert ids, unsupported alert-source values, and invalid render events? [Coverage, Gap, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §paths, Contract §security]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK022 Are edge cases defined for alerts whose selected metadata is present but upstream supporting context points to different forecast windows, mismatched timestamps, or stale lineage? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-003 through §FR-006, Plan §Service Direction, Data Model §Overview]
- [x] CHK023 Are boundary rules specified for exactly zero available components, where all supporting components are unavailable rather than failed and the UI must avoid implying a usable partial view exists? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-013a, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §AlertDetailView]
- [x] CHK024 Are concurrency and idempotency expectations defined for repeated clicks, concurrent drill-down requests for the same alert, or duplicate render-event submissions for one load record? [Edge Case, Gap, Plan §Summary, Data Model §AlertDetailLoadRecord, Contract §paths]
- [x] CHK025 Are API payload-size and visualization-boundary requirements defined for unusually dense distribution curves, large anomaly timelines, or oversized driver labels that could degrade rendering or network response size? [Edge Case, Gap, Plan §Performance Goals, Contract §DistributionComponent, Contract §AnomalyComponent, Contract §DriverContribution]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK026 Are UX requirements defined for how unavailable components, failed components, loading state, and selected-alert identity should be communicated so the drill-down remains trustworthy? [Non-Functional, UX, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-013a, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-015]
- [x] CHK027 Are data retention and auditability requirements specified for `AlertDetailLoadRecord` rows, correlation ids, failure reasons, and render-event timestamps, or is long-term observability still assumed rather than required? [Non-Functional, Data, Gap, Spec §FR-016, Plan §Technical Context, Data Model §AlertDetailLoadRecord]
- [x] CHK028 Are performance requirements quantified for backend read latency, payload assembly time, and frontend-visible completion time under normal operational load? [Non-Functional, Performance, Gap, Plan §Performance Goals, Plan §Scale/Scope]
- [x] CHK029 Are security requirements specific about role boundaries between operational managers allowed to view alert details and administrators or system actors allowed to report or inspect render outcomes? [Non-Functional, Security, Plan §Constitution Check, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §security]
- [x] CHK030 Are reliability requirements defined for how alert-detail retrieval behaves when one upstream source system is degraded but other supporting components remain accessible? [Non-Functional, Reliability, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Plan §Implementation Notes]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Are dependencies on retained alert events from UC-10 and UC-11, reusable forecast visualization context from UC-05, and anomaly-supporting lineage from UC-09 explicitly documented as requirement constraints rather than implicit design knowledge? [Dependencies, Plan §Summary, Plan §Scale/Scope, Data Model §Reused Shared Entities and Vocabulary]
- [x] CHK032 Is the assumption that only authenticated operational-manager access is in scope validated by explicit requirements, or should the spec state whether administrators and support operators share drill-down permissions? [Assumption, Spec §FR-001, Plan §API Contract Direction, Data Model §AlertDetailLoadRecord]
- [x] CHK033 Are frontend-to-backend dependency requirements defined for how and when the client must report render outcomes, including timeout handling if the render-event API cannot be reached after a successful payload load? [Dependency, Gap, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §AlertDetailRenderEvent]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK034 Is there any conflict between the contract exposing `loading` as a canonical `viewStatus` and the plan’s GET endpoint description that returns a normalized payload for a selected alert, which may imply terminal states only? [Conflict, Plan §API Contract Direction, Data Model §View Status, Contract §ViewStatus]
- [x] CHK035 Is “partial” established clearly enough in the requirements when exactly one or more components are unavailable, or does its meaning still depend more on plan and data-model rules than on the feature spec language itself? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-013a, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §AlertDetailView]
- [x] CHK036 Are the terms “alert-detail request,” “component retrieval outcome,” “preparation outcome,” “render outcome,” and “view status” used consistently enough across spec, plan, data model, and contract to support one canonical UX, API, and observability vocabulary? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Summary, Data Model §Canonical UC-12 Vocabulary, Contract §components.schemas]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline.
- This checklist is intentionally lightweight and author-oriented, with equal coverage across UX, data, performance, API, and security requirement quality.
